Title: Xanthomonas translucens commandeers the host rate-limiting step in ABA biosynthesis for disease susceptibility
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Traes_3AS_45D8C6A2A (one predicted wheat ortholog examined by qRT-PCR)
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPR1 family transcription coactivator/salicylic acid signaling regulator
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed in infected leaves with Tal8-containing Xtu or after ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced at 1 dpi in cv. Jagger and/or Chinese Spring upon WT Xtu infection or exogenous ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xtu infection; exogenous 100 μM ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq heatmap; qRT-PCR after infection and ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates stress and water-relations responses but here antagonizes SA defense and promotes susceptibility
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TaNPR1 expression is suppressed when ABA is elevated by Tal8-induced TaNCED, consistent with reduced SA perception and greater disease susceptibility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNPR1 expression decreases in wheat leaves infected with Tal8-containing Xtu. 2. Exogenous ABA also suppresses TaNPR1 expression. 3. The data support ABA-mediated antagonism of SA perception during susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced defense signaling likely increases pathogen susceptibility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining NPR1 activity or uncoupling it from ABA suppression could improve disease resistance, though not shown as direct engineering target in this paper
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; Sequence Read Archive BioProject PRJNA485724
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core downstream common gene because it was experimentally examined in Results as a metabolically relevant defense output, but it is not the primary causal ABA biosynthetic gene

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter contains sequence similarity to Tal8 EBE
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Tal8 due to promoter sequence similarity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in infected wheat tissue by Tal8
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate target prediction; expression analysis; transient promoter assay; virulence assessment showing no independent contribution
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Although induced by Tal8, TaERF_1BL showed no evidence of contributing independently to virulence under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaERF_1BL is co-induced by Tal8 because of promoter similarity to TaNCED targets. 2. It was evaluated as a potential susceptibility-related gene. 3. No evidence supported an independent role in virulence beyond NCED-mediated ABA effects.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority engineering target than TaNCED-5BS because virulence contribution was not supported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; Sequence Read Archive BioProject PRJNA485724
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary core gene for nutrient metabolism causality, but experimentally examined in Results/Discussion as an alternative Tal8 target and thus listed here as common rather than regulatory core gene

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native TaNCED-5BS promoter; TAL effector-responsive EBE-containing promoter (native, inducible by Tal8)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step from epoxycarotenoids to xanthoxin in ABA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, the direct precursor for downstream ABA formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates abiotic stress responses including drought-related stomatal closure and water loss; in this study elevated ABA promotes disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in infected wheat leaves by Xtu Tal8
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in infected wheat leaves after inoculation with Tal8-containing Xtu strains
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xanthomonas translucens pv. undulosa infection; exogenous ABA application; humidity/water relations in infected leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TAL effector mutant virulence assays; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; promoter/EBE transient GUS assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; designer TAL effector induction; ABA quantification; disease and bacterial spread phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as the rate-limiting step in ABA biosynthesis and commandeered by pathogen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induction of TaNCED-5BS increases ABA accumulation, reduces transpiration and water loss, enhances bacterial spread, and promotes disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNCED-5BS was identified as a principal wheat susceptibility gene directly induced by the Xtu TAL effector Tal8. 2. Its induction elevates ABA biosynthesis through the rate-limiting NCED step. 3. Increased ABA is associated with reduced water loss/transpiration, enhanced bacterial spread in leaves, and suppression of TaNPR1 expression, thereby increasing susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated ABA likely suppresses defense signaling and alters water relations, increasing disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter editing of the Tal8 effector-binding element in TaNCED-5BS is a promising strategy to reduce bacterial leaf streak susceptibility while minimizing disruption of endogenous ABA functions; should consider drought-response tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; Sequence Read Archive BioProject PRJNA485724
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes TaNCED-5BS is the principal targeted homeolog on chromosome 5B; TaNCED-5DS is also inducible but appears less decisive due to promoter polymorphism and lower expression/virulence contribution

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Binds native wheat promoters carrying effector-binding elements in TaNCED-5BS and TaNCED-5DS; also TaERF_1BL promoter-like site
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcription activator-like effector (TAL effector) with central repeat DNA-binding domain and type III secreted effector architecture
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Bacterial infection/type III secretion during host colonization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of host susceptibility genes via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis induction in host tissue
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TaNCED-5BS and TaNCED-5DS expression through EBE-containing promoters; indirectly suppresses TaNPR1 and PR1 expression through ABA increase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates water loss and stress responses; elevated levels increase disease susceptibility in this pathosystem
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xtu infection; humidity effects on lesion development; exogenous ABA treatment comparisons
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed by virulent Xtu strain XT4699 during infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TAL effector gene mutants; complementation/designer TAL effectors; virulence assays; promoter transient expression assays; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; ABA measurements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tal8 enhances virulence by inducing host ABA biosynthesis genes, increasing ABA accumulation and host susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive virulence effector that commandeers the host rate-limiting ABA biosynthetic step
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tal8 is the Xtu effector associated with enhanced virulence in strain XT4699. 2. It directly binds and activates wheat TaNCED promoters to elevate ABA. 3. Tal8-mediated ABA accumulation reduces transpiration/water loss, enhances bacterial spread, and suppresses defense-associated TaNPR1/PR1 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Virulence benefit may depend on host promoter compatibility and environmental humidity; no plant breeding value directly except as target for resistance deployment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use Tal8 target prediction and promoter editing of host EBEs to engineer resistance against Xtu strains carrying Tal8
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; Sequence Read Archive BioProject PRJNA485724
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tal8 is a pathogen effector rather than a plant endogenous regulator, but it is central to the demonstrated gene–metabolite causality in the paper
